name: First Aid for Fudge!
recipeInstructions:
1. If your fudge is too hard or stiffened in the saucepan, add 2 tablespoons of cream and stir over very low heat until warm and slightly softened but not hot.
2. Remove from heat and beat again until smooth and thick.
3. If your fudge did not set properly, it may be undercooked. Add 1/4 cup milk or cream and cook until the mixture reaches 236 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Cool as directed in the main recipe instructions.
5. If the fudge is too soft, transfer to a marble, tile, or formica countertop. Knead between hands like bread dough until firm enough to hold its shape.
6. Roll into a sausage shape or square about 1 inch thick.
7. Slice and serve.
8. If any leftover fudge remains, scrape it out, knead with your hands until smooth and creamy, and roll into marble-sized balls.
